---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

This is an [R Markdown](http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com) Notebook. When you execute code within the notebook, the results appear beneath the code. 

Try executing this chunk by clicking the *Run* button within the chunk or by placing your cursor inside it and pressing *Cmd+Shift+Enter*. 

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(flextable)
library(officer)
library(rlang)

# =========================
# 1. Read data
# =========================
df <- read.csv(
  "/Users/zsayali1/Documents/2004 Analysis/UpdatedBook.csv",
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
  na.strings = c("", "NA", "N/A", ".", "--")
)
df <- df %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.character), ~ trimws(.x))) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.character), ~ na_if(.x, "")))
# =========================
# 2. Recode / enforce types
# =========================
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    # Drug: 1 = Placebo, 2 = Psilocybin
    Drug = factor(Drug,
                  levels = c(1, 2),
                  labels = c("Placebo", "Psilocybin")),

    # Gender (unchanged)
        # Race (numeric → labeled factor)
    Gender = factor(Gender,
                  levels = c(1, 2, 3),
                  labels = c(
                    "Male",
                    "Female",
                    "Transgender"
                  )),
    
    Severity = factor(Severity,
                    levels = c("Mild", "Moderate", "Severe")),


    # Race (numeric → labeled factor)
    Race = factor(Race,
                  levels = c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8),
                  labels = c(
                    "White",
                    "Black",
                    "Asian",
                    "Indian",
                    "Native Hawaiian or Other Pacific Islander",
                    "Mixed",
                    "Unknown",
                    "Other"
                  )),

    # Antidepressant (unchanged)
    Antidepressant = factor(Antidepressant,
                            levels = c(0, 1),
                            labels = c("No", "Yes")),

    # Hispanic (unchanged)
    Hispanic = factor(Hispanic,
                      levels = c(0, 1),
                      labels = c("No", "Yes")),

    # Continuous variables
    Age = as.numeric(Age),
    TLFB = as.numeric(TLFB),
    GRID = as.numeric(GRID),
    DrinksPerDay = as.numeric(DrinksPerDay),
    DrinksPerHeavy = as.numeric(DrinksPerHeavy),
    Abstinent = as.numeric(Abstinent)
  )
# =========================
# 3. Helper functions
# =========================
fmt_n_pct <- function(n, d) {
  ifelse(is.na(n) | n == 0, "--", sprintf("%d (%.1f%%)", n, 100 * n / d))
}

fmt_mean_sd <- function(x) {
  sprintf("%.1f (%.1f)", mean(x, na.rm = TRUE), sd(x, na.rm = TRUE))
}

# =========================
# 4. N per treatment group
# =========================
n_by_group <- df %>%
  count(Drug, name = "N")

# =========================
# 5. Function to build categorical blocks
# =========================
make_cat_block <- function(data, var, levels_vec, row_labels, section_name, n_lookup) {
  tmp <- expand_grid(
    Drug = levels(data$Drug),
    level = levels_vec
  ) %>%
    left_join(
      data %>%
        count(Drug, !!sym(var), name = "n") %>%
        mutate(level = as.character(!!sym(var))) %>%
        select(Drug, level, n),
      by = c("Drug", "level")
    ) %>%
    left_join(n_lookup, by = "Drug") %>%
    mutate(
      n = replace_na(n, 0),
      value = fmt_n_pct(n, N),
      Variable = row_labels[match(level, levels_vec)]
    ) %>%
    select(Variable, Drug, value) %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = Drug, values_from = value)

  header <- tibble(
    Variable = section_name,
    Placebo = "",
    Psilocybin = ""
  )

  bind_rows(header, tmp)
}

# =========================
# 6. Build categorical sections
# =========================
gender_block <- make_cat_block(
  data = df,
  var = "Gender",
  levels_vec = c("Male", "Female", "Transgender"),
  row_labels = c("    Male", "    Female", "    Transgender"),
  section_name = "Gender",
  n_lookup = n_by_group
)

race_block <- make_cat_block(
  data = df,
  var = "Race",
  levels_vec = c("White", "Black", "Asian", "Indian"),
  row_labels = c(
    "    White or Caucasian",
    "    Black or African American",
    "    Asian",
    "    American Indian"
  ),
  section_name = "Race",
  n_lookup = n_by_group
)


# Ethnicity section: only show Hispanic row
ethnicity_counts <- expand_grid(
  Drug = levels(df$Drug),
  Hispanic = "Yes"
) %>%
  left_join(
    df %>% count(Drug, Hispanic, name = "n"),
    by = c("Drug", "Hispanic")
  ) %>%
  left_join(n_by_group, by = "Drug") %>%
  mutate(
    n = replace_na(n, 0),
    value = fmt_n_pct(n, N),
    Variable = "    Hispanic"
  ) %>%
  select(Variable, Drug, value) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Drug, values_from = value)

ethnicity_block <- bind_rows(
  tibble(Variable = "Ethnicity", Placebo = "", Psilocybin = ""),
  ethnicity_counts
)

sever_block <- make_cat_block(
  data = df,
  var = "Severity",
  levels_vec = c("Mild", "Moderate", "Severe"),
  row_labels = c(
    "    Mild",
    "    Moderate",
    "    Severe"
  ),
  section_name = "AUD Severity",
  n_lookup = n_by_group
)

antidepressant_block <- make_cat_block(
  data = df,
  var = "Antidepressant",
  levels_vec = c("No", "Yes"),
  row_labels = c("    No", "    Yes"),
  section_name = "Maintained on Antidepressants",
  n_lookup = n_by_group
)

# =========================
# 7. N row
# =========================
n_row <- n_by_group %>%
  transmute(Variable = "N", Drug, value = as.character(N)) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Drug, values_from = value)

# =========================
# 8. Continuous rows
# =========================
cont_rows <- df %>%
  mutate(
    pct_abstinent = ifelse(!is.na(Total) & Total > 0, (Abstinent / Total) * 100, NA_real_),
    pct_heavy_drinking = ifelse(!is.na(Total) & Total > 0, (TLFB / Total) * 100, NA_real_)
  ) %>%
  group_by(Drug) %>%
  summarise(
    `Age, mean (SD)` = fmt_mean_sd(Age),
    `GRID-HAMD, mean (SD)` = fmt_mean_sd(GRID),
    `% days abstinent` = fmt_mean_sd(pct_abstinent),
    `% heavy drinking days` = fmt_mean_sd(pct_heavy_drinking),
    `Average no. of drinks per regular (non-heavy) drinking day` = fmt_mean_sd(DrinksPerDay),
    `Average no. of drinks per heavy drinking day` = fmt_mean_sd(DrinksPerHeavy),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -Drug, names_to = "Variable", values_to = "value") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Drug, values_from = value)

# =========================
# 9. Final table body
# =========================
table_body <- bind_rows(
  n_row,
  tibble(Variable = "", Placebo = "", Psilocybin = ""),
  gender_block,
  tibble(Variable = "", Placebo = "", Psilocybin = ""),
  race_block,
  tibble(Variable = "", Placebo = "", Psilocybin = ""),
  ethnicity_block,
  tibble(Variable = "", Placebo = "", Psilocybin = ""),
  sever_block,
  tibble(Variable = "", Placebo = "", Psilocybin = ""),
  antidepressant_block,
  tibble(Variable = "", Placebo = "", Psilocybin = ""),
  cont_rows
)

print(table_body, n = Inf)

# =========================
# 10. Build flextable
# =========================
ft <- flextable(table_body)

ft <- add_header_row(
  ft,
  values = c("", "Condition"),
  colwidths = c(1, 2)
)

ft <- set_header_labels(
  ft,
  Variable = "",
  Placebo = "Placebo",
  Psilocybin = "Psilocybin"
)

ft <- merge_h(ft, part = "header")

ft <- ft %>%
  border_remove() %>%
  font(fontname = "Times New Roman", part = "all") %>%
  fontsize(size = 12, part = "all") %>%
  align(j = 1, align = "left", part = "all") %>%
  align(j = 2:3, align = "center", part = "all") %>%
  valign(valign = "center", part = "all") %>%
  padding(padding = 3, part = "all") %>%
  line_spacing(space = 1.0, part = "all")

ft <- bold(ft, part = "header", bold = TRUE)

section_rows <- which(table_body$Variable %in% c(
  "Gender",
  "Race",
  "Ethnicity",
  "AUD Severity",
  "Maintained on Antidepressants"
))

ft <- bold(ft, i = section_rows, j = 1, bold = TRUE, part = "body")

ft <- hline_top(
  ft,
  part = "header",
  border = fp_border(color = "black", width = 1)
)

ft <- hline(
  ft,
  i = 2,
  part = "header",
  border = fp_border(color = "black", width = 1)
)

ft <- hline_bottom(
  ft,
  part = "body",
  border = fp_border(color = "black", width = 1)
)

ft <- width(ft, j = 1, width = 3.8)
ft <- width(ft, j = 2, width = 1.3)
ft <- width(ft, j = 3, width = 1.3)

ft <- height_all(ft, height = 0.23)

# =========================
# 11. Export to Word
# =========================
output_path <- "/Users/zsayali1/Documents/2004 Analysis/Table1.docx"

doc <- read_docx() %>%
  body_add_flextable(ft)

print(doc, target = output_path)
```

Add a new chunk by clicking the *Insert Chunk* button on the toolbar or by pressing *Cmd+Option+I*.

When you save the notebook, an HTML file containing the code and output will be saved alongside it (click the *Preview* button or press *Cmd+Shift+K* to preview the HTML file). 

The preview shows you a rendered HTML copy of the contents of the editor. Consequently, unlike *Knit*, *Preview* does not run any R code chunks. Instead, the output of the chunk when it was last run in the editor is displayed.
